steps.title: Identify the rhetorical goal
steps.content: The student wants to "introduce the artist's 1983 poetry collection." A successful answer must (a) name the 1983 collection, Precario/Precarious, as its main subject and (b) present it as a poetry collection by Cecilia Vicuña.

steps.title: Look for a choice that foregrounds the 1983 collection
steps.content: Scan the options for one that puts Precario/Precarious (1983) front and center as the topic rather than mentioning it in passing or focusing on something else.

steps.title: Eliminate by goal violation
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Before she published the books Precario/Precarious (1983) and Instan (2002)...': the main clause is about a 1971 visual-art exhibition, not the 1983 collection; the collection is treated as background. Misses the goal. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Cecilia Vicuña is a true multidisciplinary artist...': speaks generally about the artist; never names or introduces the 1983 collection. Misses the goal. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Published in 1983 by Tanam Press, Precario/Precarious is a collection of poetry by the multidisciplinary artist Cecilia Vicuña.': makes the 1983 collection the grammatical subject and supplies its year, publisher, genre, and author. ✓
  • Choice D — 'In 1971, Cecilia Vicuña exhibited her first solo art exhibition...': introduces a 1971 visual-art exhibition, not the 1983 poetry collection. Off topic. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
